expectations.text: Identifies that hold_soc_state and its expiry timer are in-memory only and lost on restart/reload
expectations.evidence: "armed entirely in an in-memory closure dict with an in-memory expiry timer"; "a HA restart destroys both the hold record and its auto-restore timer"

expectations.text: Identifies that persist_force_mode_state serializes only force charge/discharge (Hold writes None)
expectations.evidence: "that function only serializes force_charge_state/force_discharge_state. With a hold active, state_to_save = None — it not only skips the hold, it actively writes force_mode_state = None"

expectations.text: Identifies that no startup path restores or exits a stale hold
expectations.evidence: "restore_force_mode_from_persistence() finds nothing and returns; it only understands mode in ('charge','discharge')"; "_restore_disabled_optimizer_reserve_if_stale explicitly excludes Sigenergy (line 129) and only fixes reserve, never EMS/work mode"
"With Smart Optimization OFF, no LP cycle ever overwrites STANDBY → frozen forever"

expectations.text: Fix outline includes persisting hold state with expiry and restoring/exiting on setup
expectations.evidence: "Add a hold branch to persist_force_mode_state()..."; "extend restore_force_mode_from_persistence() with mode=='hold': re-arm state + timer... call restore_normal if expired" plus a stale-STANDBY runtime guard

notes: 5/5. Independently discovered a NEW latent bug (Battery Mode sensor reads hold_soc_state from hass.data where it is never registered — now HD-13 in the registry). Cost: 41 tool uses / 510 s vs the with-skill run's 20 / 267 s.
